---
title: "Curriculum vitae"
---

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
A PDF version is available here: [Download CV (PDF)](assets/cv/ross-wardrup-cv.pdf).
Drop the file at `assets/cv/ross-wardrup-cv.pdf` to enable the link.
:::

The summary below is kept current. Dates and institution details in placeholders
should be confirmed before this is treated as final.

## Ross Wardrup

Spatial epidemiology · public-health GIS · applied statistical software

[minorsecond@gmail.com](mailto:minorsecond@gmail.com) ·
[github.com/minorsecond](https://github.com/minorsecond)

---

## Education

**MPH, Epidemiology** — University of Memphis
*In progress; expected 2027.* Quantitative thesis on West Nile virus mosquito
surveillance in California (Bayesian hierarchical spatiotemporal modeling).

**[Prior degree, field]** — [Institution], [year]
<!-- Fill in undergraduate / other degrees. -->

---

## Experience

**GIS / Software Developer — FEMA Hazus program support**
*[Employer], [start]–present*
Develop and maintain GIS-driven hazard and risk-modeling software for FEMA's
Hazus natural-hazard loss-estimation program. Work spans application code,
spatial data engineering, database maintenance, and validation. Stack: C#, C++,
SQL Server, ArcGIS-based tooling.

**MPH Epidemiology Practicum — Southeast Utah Health Department**
*2026*
Spatial analysis of respiratory illness (space-time cubes, emerging hot-spot
analysis, Getis-Ord Gi\*) and immunization-compliance reporting from EHR data.
Delivered reusable ArcGIS/`arcpy` workflows and non-technical method notes.

<!-- Add earlier roles as needed. -->

---

## Selected projects

**MESA — Mapping Epidemiology in Sparse Areas.** C++ desktop application for
public-health spatial analysis in rural/sparse settings: geocoding, Getis-Ord
Gi\*, Moran's I, and Monte Carlo permutation testing, validated against PySAL and
R. [Details](projects/mesa.qmd)

**WNV thesis pipeline.** Reproducible ETL and Bayesian spatiotemporal modeling
harness for California WNV surveillance data on a county-month grid.
[Details](projects/wnv-thesis.qmd)

---

## Skills

**Languages.** C++, C#, R, Python, SQL
**Spatial / statistical.** Bayesian hierarchical and spatiotemporal modeling
(CAR/BYM2), exploratory spatial data analysis, local spatial statistics
(Gi\*, Moran's I), permutation inference; PySAL, R spatial stack (INLA/mgcv,
sf), ArcGIS Pro / `arcpy`
**Data / infrastructure.** SQL Server, reproducible pipelines, version control,
reproducible R/Python environments

---

## Publications & presentations

See [Publications & research](publications.qmd).

---

*Last updated: 2026. Placeholder fields marked with brackets are pending
confirmation.*
